Q10 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In film and videotape, it is possible to induce viewers to project their feelings onto characters on the screen. In one study, when a camera shot of a woman’s face was preceded by a shot of a baby in a crib, the audience thought the woman’s face was registering happiness. When the same shot of the woman’s face was preceded by a shot of a lion running toward the camera, the audience thought the woman’s face was registering fear. Television news teams must be careful to avoid such manipulation of their viewers.
Which of the following can be inferred from the passage?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
This is an inference question. The test writers are probably not interested in the conclusion of the passage. Considering the given options, we’ll look for a statement that seems so obvious that it almost doesn’t need saying.
• Television news teams have abused their position of trust in the past. If you chose this answer,
you inferred too much. The passage doesn’t say that news teams have ever abused their position
of trust. Eliminate it.
• The expression on the woman’s face was, in actuality, blank. The audience had no idea what
the expression on the woman’s face was, and neither do we.
• A camera shot of a baby in a crib provoked feelings of happiness in the audience. This is the
best answer. The passage says that the audience projects its own feelings onto characters on
the screen. If the audience believes the woman’s face reflects happiness, then that must have
been its own reaction.
• Audiences should strive to be less gullible. Rather it suggests Television news teams to be
careful to avoid manipulation of their viewers. This statement goes way beyond the intent of the passage.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

Q25 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following Python class and function:

 
For a given linked list p, the function f returns TRUE if and only if:
Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
For a given linked list p, the function returns TRUE if and only if the elements in the list are sorted
in non-decreasing order of the data value.
The function returns TRUE when p is None or p.next is None
But the term ”if and only if” makes option (a) false because the function also returns TRUE for
linked lists having more than one node, as long as the data values are in non-decreasing order.
Hence, the function returns true exactly when the list is sorted in non-decreasing order.
So, option (b) is the correct answer.

Q27 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about discretization:
S1 : Equal-frequency binning ensures that each bin contains approximately the same number of data points, even if the interval widths differ.
S2 : In unsupervised discretization, bin boundaries are determined without using class labels.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Equal-frequency binning divides the dataset such that each bin has roughly the same number
of points. Interval widths may differ depending on the data distribution. So, S1 is correct.
• Unsupervised discretization does not use class labels. Bin boundaries are determined solely
based on the data values (e.g., equal-width or equal-frequency methods). So, S2 is correct.

Q28 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following sorting algorithms has the lowest time complexity for sorting a random linked list?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Linked lists can employ both merge sort and insertion sort. The algorithms, such quicksort, perform
poorly because of a linked list’s terrible random-access performance, while heapsort is impossible
because of linked list. Merge sort is preferable over Insertion sort because its worst-case time
complexity is Ο(n log n) and insertion sorts worst case time complexity is Ο(n2).

Q29 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Let G = (V, E) be an undirected connected simple (i.e., no parallel edges or self-loops) graph with the weight function w : E → R on its edge set. Let w(e1) < w(e2) < ..... < w(em), where E = {e1, e2, ....., em} and m ≥ 2. Suppose T is a minimum spanning tree of G. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
(Here every edge weight is distinct, therefore MST is unique).
Option (a) is True. If we apply Kruskal’s algorithm, it will choose e1.
Option (b) is True. If we apply Kruskal’s algorithm then it will also choose e2, and 2 edges can not
form a cycle. (e3 is not guaranteed in MST, as it may form a cycle)
Option (c) is also true. If we apply Prim’s algorithm also on any vertex (say u), it chooses minimum
weight edge incident on vertex u.
Option (d) is true. Because every edge weight is distinct.

Q35 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In a ridge regression model, the regularization parameter λ is gradually increased from 0 to a very large value.
Which of the following effects are most likely to occur?
(i) The model coefficients approach zero.
(ii) The model bias decreases or remains constant.
(iii) The model variance decreases.
(iv) The model overfitting tendency increases.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• As λ increases, the penalty on large weights becomes stronger.
Hence, the coefficients are forced to shrink toward zero to minimize the cost function. So, (i) is correct.
• A larger λ makes the model simpler and less flexible.
This causes bias to increase, not decrease, since the model underfits the data. So, (ii) is incorrect.
• Increasing λ stabilizes the model by reducing sensitivity to training data.
Hence, the variance decreases because the model's predictions become more consistent. So, (iii) is correct.
• Regularization suppresses overly complex models and prevents overfitting.
Therefore, overfitting decreases, not increases, when λ grows. So, (iv) is incorrect.

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following techniques do not perform feature selection?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
(a) PCA (Principal Component Analysis): PCA reduces dimensionality by transforming correlated
variables into a smaller set of uncorrelated components. It performs feature extraction and
implicit selection of principal components based on explained variance.
(b) Naive Bayes Classifier: It assumes independence among features and uses all of them to
compute class probabilities. It does not perform feature selection - no feature is removed or
weighted differently.
(c) Decision Tree: A Decision Tree selects features based on the highest information gain or
lowest Gini impurity. Only the most informative features are used for splits, hence it performs
feature selection inherently.
(d) K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN): KNN is a non-parametric, instance-based learning algorithm.
It uses all features to compute distances between points, so it does not perform feature selection.

Q40 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S2 : Selection Sort always performs the minimum possible number of swaps compared to Bubble Sort.
S2 : Bubble Sort is a stable sorting algorithm.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Selection Sort finds the minimum element and swaps it into place.
It performs at most (n – 1) swaps, which is the minimum required to place all elements in correct positions.
Bubble Sort, on the other hand: Uses repeated adjacent swaps.
Can perform up to Ο(n2) swaps in the worst case. Therefore, S1 is correct.
• Bubble Sort is stable because it only swaps adjacent elements when they are in the wrong
order. If two equal elements appear, Bubble Sort does NOT change their relative order.
Therefore, S2 is correct.

Q41 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

There is an integer array nums sorted in ascending order (with distinct values). Prior to being passed to your function, nums is possibly rotated at an unknown pivot index k (1 ≤ k < nums.length) such that the resulting array is [nums[k], nums[k + 1], ..., nums[n – 1], nums[0], nums[1], ...,
nums[k – 1]] (0-indexed). For example, [0, 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7] might be rotated at pivot index 3 and become [4, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2]. Given the array nums after the possible rotation and an integer target, return the index of target if it is in nums, or –1 if it is not in nums. Let the time complexity of the efficient algorithm to solve this problem be Ο(na logb n). Then the value of a + b is?

NAT
Correct answer: 1
Solution

1
We can apply binary search, when the array is rotated:
Case I: The MID is in the LEFT subarray
The target is between the (MID, LEFT] → Only look at LEFT of MID → R = mid – 1
Else only look at RIGHT of mid → L = mid + 1
Case II: The MID is in the RIGHT subarray
The target is between the (MID, RIGHT] → Only look at RIGHT of mid → L = mid + 1
Else only look at LEFT of mid → R = mid-1

Q50 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

For start pointing to the first node of the following linked list:
10 → 20 → 30 → 40 → 50 → 60
What will be the sum of all numbers printed by the following function?

 
NAT
Correct answer: 180
Solution

180


The function first prints the data of the current node, then recursively calls itself by skipping one node (start.next.next).
Because of the semicolon-like behavior, the recursive call executes every time, even when start.next is not None.
After the recursive call returns, it prints the current node's data again.
Hence, only alternate nodes (10, 30, 50) are printed twice — once before and once after recursion.
So the output is 10 30 50 50 30 10, and the sum of printed values is 180.

Q52 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

For each of the following statements, say whether it is always true or not.
Statement 1: Suppose A is an m × n matrix such that  can be solved for any choice of 

Then the columns of A form a basis for
Statement 2: The set of polynomials of degree ≤ 5 forms a vector space.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Statement 1 simply tells us Solution exist, but for A matrix columns to form a basis one more
condition is required. All columns must be Linearly independent. This is not mentioned. So not always true.
• Statement 2 is true: You should check that the set of polynomials of degree ≤ 5 satisfies all the
rules for being a vector space. Since this is a subset of the collection of all polynomials (which
we know is a vector space) all you really need to check is that this collection is closed under
addition and scalar multiplication.

Q57 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about the Snowflake schema is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• Snowflake schema normalizes dimension tables, which reduces redundancy. So, option (a) is correct.
• Fact tables are typically not normalized; normalization is applied to dimensions. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• While Snowflake schemas often require more joins, in some scenarios, normalization reduces
data size and improves indexing, which can improve query performance. So, option (c) is correct.
• Foreign key relationships are required to link fact and dimension tables. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q58 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the aforementioned claims concerning normal forms is/are TRUE?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
In some cases, it is not possible to decompose a table in BCNF while maintaining dependencies.
For instance, the functional dependencies “AB → C, C → B” cannot be broken down in the BCNF
model.
